docs.ti: yom hakippurim (Day of Atonement, Day of Coverings)
docs.tk: yom_hakippurim yom hakippurim day of atonement  day of coverings the most solemn day in the israelite calendar  lev 16  23 26-32   observed on the 10th of tishri  the kohen gadol performs a unique sequence  entering the holy of holies with incense smoke  to avoid seeing god's glory and dying   sprinkling blood on the kapporet  then sending the azazel goat into the wilderness  this annual ritual cleanses the entire tabernacle system from accumulated impurity  the writer of hebrews argues that yom kippur was a shadow of christ's once-for-all atonement  jesus entered the true heavenly holy of holies with his own blood  not the blood of goats  heb 9 11-14  24-28   the azazel goat connection to 1 enoch's imprisoned watcher adds a divine council dimension  sin is returned to its supernatural source
docs.sn: The most solemn day in the Israelite calendar (Lev 16, 23:26-32), observed on the 10th of Tishri. The kohen gadol performs a unique sequence: entering

docs.ti: reach nichoach (pleasing aroma, soothing fragrance)
docs.tk: reach_nichoach reach nichoach pleasing aroma  soothing fragrance the technical phrase for god's acceptance of a sacrifice  lev 1 9  13  17  gen 8 21   not meant literally  god does not 'smell'   this is anthropomorphic language   the reach nichoach signals that the offering has been received and the worshiper's approach accepted  the same phrase appears in the flood narrative when noah sacrifices  gen 8 21   creating continuity between pre-sinai and levitical worship  paul applies it to christ in ephesians 5 2  'christ loved us and gave himself up for us  a fragrant offering and sacrifice to god ' in ane parallels  the gilgamesh epic describes the gods gathering 'like flies over the sacrifice'   a stark contrast to the biblical portrayal of the sovereign god graciously choosing to accept
docs.sn: The technical phrase for God's acceptance of a sacrifice (Lev 1:9, 13, 17; Gen 8:21). NOT meant literally (God does not 'smell' — this is anthropomorp

docs.ti: nachash nechoshet (bronze serpent, serpent of brass)
docs.tk: nachash_nechoshet nachash nechoshet bronze serpent  serpent of brass the bronze serpent moses made at god's command  num 21 8-9  after yhwh sent seraphim  nachashim seraphim   'fiery serpents'  among the people  the wordplay is extraordinary  nachash  'serpent shining one'  made of nechoshet  'bronze copper'    both from the same root n-ch-sh  the serpent on the pole is a deliberate callback to genesis 3  the creature that brought death is now the instrument of healing  jesus explicitly identifies this as a type of his crucifixion  'as moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness  so must the son of man be lifted up'  john 3 14   in divine council theology  the nachash of eden was a shining divine being  here  a bronze representation of that being becomes the means of salvation   foreshadowing how christ takes on the form of sinful flesh to destroy sin's power  rom 8 3   king hezekiah later destroyed it when israel began worshiping it as 'nehushtan'  2 kings 18 4
docs.sn: The bronze serpent Moses made at God's command (Num 21:8-9) after YHWH sent seraphim (nachashim seraphim — 'fiery serpents') among the people. The wor

docs.ti: nazir (one set apart, Nazirite, consecrated one)
docs.tk: nazir nazir one set apart  nazirite  consecrated one from nazar  'to separate  consecrate'   the nazirite vow  num 6 1-21  involves three abstentions  no grape products  wine  vinegar  grapes  raisins   no haircuts  and no contact with the dead  this triple separation mirrors the holiness of the high priest  making the nazirite a temporary 'layperson's priesthood ' samson  judges 13-16   samuel  1 sam 1 11   and john the baptist  luke 1 15  were lifelong nazirites  the growing hair symbolizes consecrated strength  the grape prohibition symbolizes self-control and alertness before god  in the qumran community  some scholars believe the sectarians lived under quasi-nazirite regulations permanently
docs.sn: From nazar ('to separate, consecrate'). The Nazirite vow (Num 6:1-21) involves three abstentions: no grape products (wine, vinegar, grapes, raisins),

docs.ti: birkat kohanim (priestly blessing, Aaronic benediction)
docs.tk: birkat_kohanim birkat kohanim priestly blessing  aaronic benediction the three-line blessing of numbers 6 24-26  'yhwh bless you and keep you  yhwh make his face shine upon you and be gracious to you  yhwh lift up his face upon you and give you peace ' this is the oldest biblical text found archaeologically   the ketef hinnom silver scrolls  c  600 bc  bear this blessing  predating the dead sea scrolls by 400 years  the three lines form an ascending pattern of intimacy  protection   illumination   presence  the 'shining face' invokes the kavod  glory-presence  that fills the divine throne room  still recited in synagogues today  this blessing carries the weight of 3 000 years of continuous liturgical use
docs.sn: The three-line blessing of Numbers 6:24-26: 'YHWH bless you and keep you; YHWH make his face shine upon you and be gracious to you; YHWH lift up his f

docs.ti: 'anak / 'anaqim (Anak, the Anakim (giant clan))
docs.tk: anak 'anak   'anaqim anak  the anakim  giant clan  a clan of giants descended from anak  dwelling in hebron and the hill country of canaan  the 10 spies report  'we saw the nephilim   the sons of anak  from the nephilim   and we seemed like grasshoppers'  num 13 33   this is the critical link between pre-flood nephilim  gen 6 4  and post-flood giant clans  the anakim are connected to the rephaim  deut 2 11  and appear alongside other giant peoples  emim in moab  deut 2 10   zamzummim in ammon  deut 2 20   joshua later drives them from hebron but they survive in gaza  gath  and ashdod  josh 11 22    explaining why goliath of gath appears centuries later  1 sam 17   the root 'anak may mean 'long-necked' or 'necklace-wearer '
docs.sn: A clan of giants descended from Anak, dwelling in Hebron and the hill country of Canaan. The 10 spies report: 'We saw the Nephilim — the sons of Anak,

docs.ti: meraggelim (spies, scouts)
docs.tk: meraggelim meraggelim spies  scouts from ragal  'to walk about  spy out'   the 12 tribal leaders sent to reconnoiter canaan  num 13   ten returned with a 'bad report'  dibbah ra'ah    they saw the nephilim-descended anakim and concluded israel could not conquer the land  only joshua and caleb dissented  the meraggelim's sin was not in reporting facts but in interpreting them without faith  they judged by military capacity rather than by god's promise  their punishment   40 years of wandering  one year for each day of the 40-day mission   became paradigmatic for divine judgment on unbelief  heb 3 7-19  1 cor 10 1-12   the episode reveals that the giant clans remain a spiritual threat  not merely a military one
docs.sn: From ragal ('to walk about, spy out'). The 12 tribal leaders sent to reconnoiter Canaan (Num 13). Ten returned with a 'bad report' (dibbah ra'ah) — th

docs.ti: kokhav (star)
docs.tk: kokhav kokhav star in balaam's oracle  num 24 17   'a star  kokhav  shall come out of jacob  and a scepter  shevet  shall rise out of israel ' this became one of the most important messianic texts in second temple judaism  the qumran community cited it in cd vii 18-21 and 4q175  simon bar kokhba  'son of the star'  took his name from this verse during the second jewish revolt  132-135 ad   in the divine council framework  the 'star' imagery connects to the heavenly realm   stars are associated with divine beings  job 38 7  'the morning stars sang together and all the b'nei elohim shouted for joy'  isa 14 12-13  the helel ben-shachar aspires to set his throne above the stars of god   the messianic star is thus a divine figure descending from the heavenly court
docs.sn: In Balaam's oracle (Num 24:17): 'A star (kokhav) shall come out of Jacob, and a scepter (shevet) shall rise out of Israel.' This became one of the mos

docs.ti: parah adummah (red heifer)
docs.tk: parah_adummah parah adummah red heifer a completely red cow without blemish  never yoked  sacrificed outside the camp and burned entirely to ash  num 19   the ashes mixed with water create the 'water of purification'  mei niddah  for cleansing corpse contamination   the most severe form of ritual impurity  this is the most mysterious ritual in the torah  the person who prepares it becomes unclean  while the unclean person it purifies becomes clean  the rabbis called it the quintessential chok  statute beyond human reason   hebrews 9 13-14 argues that if the ashes of a heifer sanctify the flesh  'how much more will the blood of christ purify our conscience ' in modern israel  the search for a qualifying red heifer generates intense eschatological speculation  as the ashes are required for purifying a future temple
docs.sn: A completely red cow without blemish, never yoked, sacrificed outside the camp and burned entirely to ash (Num 19). The ashes mixed with water create

docs.ti: ba'al pe'or (Baal of Peor, lord of the opening)
docs.tk: baal_peor ba'al pe'or baal of peor  lord of the opening the territorial deity of moab worshiped at mount peor  num 25 1-9   israel 'yoked itself'  tzamad  to baal-peor through sexual rites with moabite women   a deliberate violation of the covenant  this is the deuteronomy 32 8 worldview in action  a territorial 'elohim seducing yhwh's portion away from him  balaam  unable to curse israel directly  apparently advised balak to use moabite women to lure israel into apostasy  num 31 16  rev 2 14   the plague killed 24 000 and was stopped only by phinehas's zealous act  hosea 9 10 remembers this as israel's deepest shame  'they came to baal-peor and consecrated themselves to the thing of shame ' the incident becomes a permanent warning against syncretism with the allotted nations' gods
docs.sn: The territorial deity of Moab worshiped at Mount Peor (Num 25:1-9). Israel 'yoked itself' (tzamad) to Baal-Peor through sexual rites with Moabite wome

docs.ti: sar tseva' YHWH (commander of YHWH's army)
docs.tk: sar_tseva sar tseva' yhwh commander of yhwh's army the divine warrior who appears to joshua near jericho with a drawn sword  josh 5 13-15   joshua asks 'are you for us or for our enemies ' and receives the stunning answer  'no   i am the commander of the army of yhwh ' he commands joshua to remove his sandals  echoing moses at the burning bush  exod 3 5   marking this as a theophany  the title 'sar tseva' yhwh' parallels daniel's 'michael  one of the chief princes  sarim '  dan 10 13   this figure leads the heavenly host   the divine council's military wing   into battle alongside israel  the conquest is fought simultaneously on earth and in the heavenly realm
docs.sn: The divine warrior who appears to Joshua near Jericho with a drawn sword (Josh 5:13-15). Joshua asks 'Are you for us or for our enemies?' and receives

docs.ti: rachav (Rahab (the woman), broad/wide)
docs.tk: rahab_name rachav rahab  the woman   broad wide the canaanite woman of jericho who hid the israelite spies  josh 2   her name rachav means 'broad wide' and is distinct from  but resonant with  the chaos monster rahab  rahab  h7294    the primordial sea dragon of psalm 89 10 and isaiah 51 9  this wordplay may be intentional  a woman named after cosmic chaos becomes an agent of yhwh's cosmic order  she confesses yhwh as 'god in heaven above and on earth beneath'  josh 2 11    a divine council confession  she is saved by the scarlet cord  passover blood typology   enters israel's genealogy  and becomes an ancestor of david and jesus  matt 1 5   james 2 25 and hebrews 11 31 both celebrate her faith
docs.sn: The Canaanite woman of Jericho who hid the Israelite spies (Josh 2). Her name rachav means 'broad/wide' and is distinct from (but resonant with) the c

docs.ti: goral (lot, portion, destiny)
docs.tk: goral goral lot  portion  destiny the 'lot' cast to determine divine will   used extensively in joshua's land allotment  josh 14 2  18 6-10   'the lot is cast into the lap  but its every decision is from yhwh'  prov 16 33   casting lots was a recognized means of accessing divine council decisions  the urim and thummim were sacred lots carried by the high priest  in joshua  the goral distributes land at shiloh before yhwh   making the allotment a divine council decree  not a human negotiation  the concept carries into the nt  the soldiers cast lots for jesus' garments  ps 22 18 matt 27 35   and matthias is chosen by lot  acts 1 26   the qumran community called themselves 'sons of the lot of god'  goral 'el
docs.sn: The 'lot' cast to determine divine will — used extensively in Joshua's land allotment (Josh 14:2, 18:6-10). 'The lot is cast into the lap, but its eve

docs.ti: selah (pause, reflect (liturgical marker))
docs.tk: selah selah pause  reflect  liturgical marker  a liturgical or musical notation appearing 71 times in the psalms and 3 times in habakkuk  3 3  9  13   its exact meaning is debated  but the most widely accepted interpretations include   1  a musical interlude or instrumental break  from the root salal  'to lift up'    perhaps lifting up voices or instruments   2  a call for the congregation to pause and reflect on what was just sung or read   3  a marker for a change in musical dynamics or key  the lxx renders it diapsalma  'interlude'   the targum translates it as 'forever'  le-almin   its consistent placement at moments of theological weight   after declarations of god's sovereignty  cries for deliverance  or statements of trust   suggests it functioned as an invitation to contemplative silence  allowing the hearer to absorb the gravity of the words  selah may be one of the oldest performance instructions in human history  predating greek theatrical notation by centuries
docs.sn: A liturgical or musical notation appearing 71 times in the Psalms and 3 times in Habakkuk (3:3, 9, 13). Its exact meaning is debated, but the most wid

docs.ti: tehillah (praise, song of praise)
docs.tk: tehillah tehillah praise  song of praise from the root halal  'to praise  to shine'   tehillah is the hebrew title of the book of psalms  sefer tehillim  'book of praises'   distinct from other praise words  todah is thanksgiving for what god has done  zamar is musical praise  halal is exuberant  even reckless praise  tehillah is the formal hymn of praise   the composed  deliberate declaration of god's character and deeds  psalm 22 3 says god is 'enthroned upon the praises  tehillot  of israel'   the praise of god's people creates the very throne room of the divine council on earth
docs.sn: From the root halal ('to praise, to shine'). Tehillah is the Hebrew title of the Book of Psalms (Sefer Tehillim, 'Book of Praises'). Distinct from oth

docs.ti: mizmor (psalm, song with instrumental accompaniment)
docs.tk: mizmor mizmor psalm  song with instrumental accompaniment from zamar  'to sing  to make music with instruments'   a mizmor is specifically a song meant to be accompanied by stringed instruments  kinnor lyre  nevel harp   it appears in the superscriptions of 57 psalms  distinct from shir  a general song  and maskil  a contemplative or skillful composition   the mizmor tradition reflects the levitical worship system established by david  1 chr 25 1-7   where trained musicians led the congregation in praise before the ark   a deliberate earthly mirror of the heavenly worship before god's throne  rev 4-5
docs.sn: From zamar ('to sing, to make music with instruments'). A mizmor is specifically a song meant to be accompanied by stringed instruments (kinnor/lyre,

docs.ti: yaḥad (community, unity)
docs.tk: yahad yaḥad community  unity hebrew noun meaning 'togetherness' or 'community ' in the dead sea scrolls  the yahad  ha-yahad  'the community'  is the self-designation of the qumran sect  the community rule  1qs  outlines how members must live in radical purity  share possessions  study torah continuously  and follow the teacher of righteousness  the yahad saw itself as the true remnant of israel  living in covenant faithfulness during the final age before god's eschatological intervention  entry required a two-year probation and binding oath  the concept echoes deuteronomy 6 4  yhwh is 'one'   echad  and anticipates the early church's 'one body' theology  acts 2 44-47  4 32
docs.sn: Hebrew noun meaning 'togetherness' or 'community.' In the Dead Sea Scrolls, the Yahad (ha-yahad, 'the community') is the self-designation of the Qumra

docs.ti: maśkîl (instructor, wise one, contemplative psalm)
docs.tk: maskil maśkîl instructor  wise one  contemplative psalm from sakal  'to be prudent  to have insight'   in the psalms  maskil appears in 13 superscriptions  psalms 32  42  44  45  52-55  74  78  88  89  142   designating a contemplative or didactic composition requiring special skill  in the dead sea scrolls  the maskil is a specific leadership role within the yahad community   the chief instructor responsible for teaching members the 'mysteries of god'  razei el   discerning the spirits of light and darkness within people  1qs 3 13-4 26   and guiding the community in righteousness  the maskil knows the 'epochs of history' and god's plan for the end times  this role bridges the wisdom tradition of proverbs  the sage who instructs  with the apocalyptic tradition of daniel  the wise who 'shine like stars ' dan 12 3
docs.sn: From sakal ('to be prudent, to have insight'). In the Psalms, maskil appears in 13 superscriptions (Psalms 32, 42, 44, 45, 52-55, 74, 78, 88, 89, 142)

docs.ti: hôdāyôt (thanksgiving hymns)
docs.tk: hodayot hôdāyôt thanksgiving hymns plural of hodayah  'thanksgiving'   the hodayot  1qh  is a collection of approximately 25 thanksgiving hymns found among the dead sea scrolls  many beginning with 'i thank you  o lord'  odeka adonai   likely composed by the teacher of righteousness and other yahad leaders  these hymns express profound spiritual experience  the author's sense of sinfulness and dust-like nature before god's overwhelming holiness  gratitude for divine election and revelation  and persecution by enemies  the theology emphasizes radical grace   that a 'creature of clay' can know divine mysteries only through god's sovereign gift of the holy spirit  this anticipates paul's emphasis on grace  eph 2 8-9  and the language of spiritual rebirth  john 3 5-8   the hodayot are the most intimate devotional texts from second temple judaism
docs.sn: Plural of hodayah ('thanksgiving'). The Hodayot (1QH) is a collection of approximately 25 thanksgiving hymns found among the Dead Sea Scrolls, many be

docs.ti: môreh tsedeq (Teacher of Righteousness)
docs.tk: moreh_tzedek môreh tsedeq teacher of righteousness title of the founding leader of the qumran community  the teacher of righteousness  moreh ha-tsedek  appears in the damascus document  cd   the habakkuk pesher  1qphab   and other scrolls as a priest to whom god revealed the true meaning of the prophets  he was persecuted by the 'wicked priest'  likely a hasmonean high priest  and led his followers into exile at qumran  the pesher on habakkuk 2 4 interprets 'the righteous shall live by his faith' as referring specifically to those who trust the teacher's interpretation of torah  his role as the authoritative interpreter of scripture parallels jesus's claim in the sermon on the mount  'you have heard it said    but i say to you'  matt 5 21-48   the teacher's identity remains debated  but most scholars date him to the mid-2nd century bc
docs.sn: Title of the founding leader of the Qumran community. The Teacher of Righteousness (Moreh ha-Tsedek) appears in the Damascus Document (CD), the Habakk

docs.ti: kohēn hārāshāʿ (the Wicked Priest)
docs.tk: kohen_rasha kohēn hārāshāʿ the wicked priest antagonist figure in the dead sea scrolls who persecuted the teacher of righteousness  the wicked priest  ha-kohen ha-rasha  appears primarily in the habakkuk pesher  1qphab   where he is described as initially righteous but corrupted by wealth and power  defiling the temple  and pursuing the teacher on the day of atonement  most scholars identify him with one of the hasmonean high priests  jonathan or simon maccabee  mid-2nd century bc   who combined political and priestly authority in ways the qumran community considered illegitimate  the wicked priest's corruption of the priesthood is precisely what drove the yahad into desert exile  fulfilling isaiah 40 3  'prepare in the wilderness the way of the lord '
docs.sn: Antagonist figure in the Dead Sea Scrolls who persecuted the Teacher of Righteousness. The Wicked Priest (ha-Kohen ha-Rasha) appears primarily in the

docs.ti: pĕshārîm (interpretations, prophetic commentaries)
docs.tk: pesharim pĕshārîm interpretations  prophetic commentaries plural of pesher  the pesharim are a distinctive genre of dead sea scrolls commentary that interprets biblical prophecy as speaking directly about the qumran community's own time and experiences  the most famous are the habakkuk pesher  1qphab   the nahum pesher  4qpnah   and the psalms pesher  4qppsᵃ   the interpretive method works verse-by-verse  quote a biblical line  then explain 'its pesher concerns   ' applying it to specific events  people  and the eschatological drama  this 'this is that' hermeneutic directly parallels how the nt authors read the ot   peter at pentecost  acts 2 16  'this is what was spoken by the prophet joel'   matthew's fulfillment citations  and paul's typological readings  the pesharim assume that the prophets wrote in mystery  and only the divinely guided interpreter  the teacher of righteousness  could unlock their true meaning
docs.sn: Plural of pesher. The pesharim are a distinctive genre of Dead Sea Scrolls commentary that interprets biblical prophecy as speaking directly about the

docs.ti: shtēy rûḥôt (two spirits (of truth and falsehood))
docs.tk: two_spirits shtēy rûḥôt two spirits  of truth and falsehood  central doctrine of the qumran community outlined in the community rule  1qs 3 13-4 26   god created two spirits that govern human behavior  the spirit of truth  ruach emet  and the spirit of falsehood  ruach avel   every person is influenced by both spirits in varying proportions until the eschatological 'visitation' when god will purify the righteous and destroy wickedness forever  the spirit of truth leads to humility  patience  compassion  and eternal joy  the spirit of falsehood leads to greed  deceit  cruelty  and destruction  this dualistic framework profoundly influenced johannine theology  'the spirit of truth and the spirit of error'  1 john 4 6   jesus as 'the light of the world' versus 'walking in darkness'  john 8 12   and the paraclete as the 'spirit of truth'  john 14 17  15 26  16 13
docs.sn: Central doctrine of the Qumran community outlined in the Community Rule (1QS 3:13-4:26). God created two spirits that govern human behavior: the Spiri

docs.ti: kanōn (rule, standard; authoritative list of Scripture)
docs.tk: canon kanōn rule  standard  authoritative list of scripture from greek kanon  measuring rod standard   the authoritative list of books recognized as scripture  the process of recognizing canonical books was gradual  spanning centuries   not a single decree  the hebrew bible was largely settled by the 2nd century bc  torah  prophets  writings   the nt canon was debated through the 4th century  with athanasius's 367 ad festal letter being the first to list the exact 27 nt books  different christian traditions  protestant  catholic  orthodox  ethiopian  recognize different ot canons
docs.sn: From Greek kanon (measuring rod/standard). The authoritative list of books recognized as Scripture. The process of recognizing canonical books was gra

docs.ti: deuterokanōn (second canon; additional canonical books)
docs.tk: deuterocanon deuterokanōn second canon  additional canonical books literally 'second canon'   books included in catholic and orthodox canons but not the protestant bible  includes tobit  judith  wisdom of solomon  sirach  ecclesiasticus   baruch  letter of jeremiah  1-2 maccabees  and additions to daniel and esther  these books were part of the septuagint  greek ot  used by the early church  but were not in the hebrew bible  jerome distinguished them in his vulgate translation  and the council of trent  1546  formally defined the catholic 73-book canon including these works
docs.sn: Literally 'second canon' — books included in Catholic and Orthodox canons but not the Protestant Bible. Includes Tobit, Judith, Wisdom of Solomon, Sir

docs.ti: apokryphos (hidden, concealed; non-canonical religious writings)
docs.tk: apocrypha apokryphos hidden  concealed  non-canonical religious writings from greek apokryphos  hidden concealed   writings of disputed or secondary canonical status  protestant usage  the deuterocanonical books  tobit  wisdom  sirach  baruch  1-2 maccabees  etc    broader usage  any non-canonical religious writing  including the gospel of thomas  the acts of peter  and other texts excluded from all major canons  the term originally implied esoteric  hidden knowledge  over time it came to mean 'of doubtful authenticity ' luther placed these books between the ot and nt as useful but not canonical
docs.sn: From Greek apokryphos (hidden/concealed). Writings of disputed or secondary canonical status. Protestant usage: the Deuterocanonical books (Tobit, Wis

docs.ti: Symbolon Nikaias (statement of faith from the Council of Nicaea)
docs.tk: nicene_creed symbolon nikaias statement of faith from the council of nicaea statement of faith produced by the council of nicaea  325 ad   later expanded at constantinople  381 ad   affirms the trinity and christ's full deity against the arian heresy  which taught that the son was a created being  key phrase  'begotten  not made  of one substance  homoousios  with the father ' the creed became the foundational confession of orthodox christianity across catholic  orthodox  and most protestant traditions  the later western addition of the filioque clause  'and the son'  became a major cause of the great schism
docs.sn: Statement of faith produced by the Council of Nicaea (325 AD), later expanded at Constantinople (381 AD). Affirms the Trinity and Christ's full deity

docs.ti: ex cathedra (doctrine that the Pope cannot err when speaking officially on faith/morals)
docs.tk: papal_infallibility ex cathedra doctrine that the pope cannot err when speaking officially on faith morals catholic doctrine formally defined at vatican i  1870  that when the pope speaks ex cathedra  'from the chair'    that is  in his official capacity as supreme pastor on matters of faith and morals   he is preserved from error by divine assistance  this does not mean everything a pope says is infallible  it applies only to solemn  definitive pronouncements intended to bind the whole church  it has been formally invoked only once since its definition  the 1950 dogma of the assumption of mary  critics note that popes have contradicted each other historically and that this doctrine has no basis in the earliest centuries of christianity
docs.sn: Catholic doctrine formally defined at Vatican I (1870) that when the Pope speaks ex cathedra ('from the chair') — that is, in his official capacity as

docs.ti: transsubstantiatio (bread and wine become Christ's body and blood)
docs.tk: transubstantiation transsubstantiatio bread and wine become christ's body and blood catholic doctrine that during the eucharist  the bread and wine literally become the body and blood of christ while retaining the outward appearances  accidents  of bread and wine  formally defined at the fourth lateran council  1215  using aristotelian categories of substance and accidents  reaffirmed at the council of trent  1551  against protestant challenges  based on a literal reading of jesus' words 'this is my body'  matthew 26 26   protestants variously hold to consubstantiation  luther   christ present 'in  with  and under' the elements   spiritual presence  calvin   or memorial view  zwingli
docs.sn: Catholic doctrine that during the Eucharist, the bread and wine literally become the body and blood of Christ while retaining the outward appearances

docs.ti: purgatorium (state of purification after death)
docs.tk: purgatory purgatorium state of purification after death catholic belief in a state of purification after death for souls destined for heaven but not yet fully cleansed of the effects of sin  souls in purgatory undergo temporal punishment before entering god's presence  the primary scriptural basis is 2 maccabees 12 46  prayers for the dead 'that they might be delivered from their sin'  and inferences from 1 corinthians 3 15  'saved  but only as through fire'   the doctrine was systematized by medieval scholastic theologians and formally affirmed at the councils of florence  1439  and trent  1563   protestants reject purgatory as unbiblical  citing hebrews 9 27 and the sufficiency of christ's atonement
docs.sn: Catholic belief in a state of purification after death for souls destined for heaven but not yet fully cleansed of the effects of sin. Souls in purgat

docs.ti: magisterium (the official teaching authority of the Catholic Church)
docs.tk: magisterium magisterium the official teaching authority of the catholic church the official teaching authority of the roman catholic church  exercised by the pope and bishops in communion with him  catholics hold the magisterium as the authentic interpreter of both scripture and sacred tradition  it operates at two levels  the ordinary magisterium  everyday teaching by bishops  and the extraordinary magisterium  solemn definitions by ecumenical councils or ex cathedra papal pronouncements   the magisterium claims authority from christ's commission to the apostles  matthew 28 18-20  and the promise of the spirit's guidance  john 16 13   protestants reject this claim  holding that scripture interprets scripture  sola scriptura
docs.sn: The official teaching authority of the Roman Catholic Church, exercised by the Pope and bishops in communion with him. Catholics hold the Magisterium

docs.ti: successio apostolica (unbroken chain of authority from the apostles through bishops)
docs.tk: apostolic_succession successio apostolica unbroken chain of authority from the apostles through bishops doctrine held by catholic and orthodox churches that spiritual authority passes from the original apostles through an unbroken chain of ordained bishops via the laying on of hands  central to catholic and orthodox ecclesiology   without valid apostolic succession  sacraments are considered invalid  claimed basis  paul's charge to timothy  2 timothy 2 2  and the appointment of elders  titus 1 5   protestant critique  the nt evidence shows 'elders'  presbyteroi  and 'bishops'  episkopoi  were the same office  acts 20 17  28  titus 1 5-7   and the monarchical episcopate developed later  not from the apostles directly
docs.sn: Doctrine held by Catholic and Orthodox churches that spiritual authority passes from the original apostles through an unbroken chain of ordained bisho

docs.ti: sola Scriptura (Scripture alone as the final rule of faith)
docs.tk: sola_scriptura sola scriptura scripture alone as the final rule of faith latin  'scripture alone ' the protestant principle that the bible is the sole infallible rule of faith and practice  this does not mean scripture is the only authority  protestants value creeds  confessions  and church tradition  but that it is the final and supreme authority to which all other authorities must submit  central to the reformation   luther at the diet of worms  1521   'unless i am convinced by scripture and plain reason    my conscience is captive to the word of god ' catholic critique  the bible doesn't teach sola scriptura  and the canon itself was determined by the church  protestant response  scripture is self-authenticating  and the church recognized  not created  the canon
docs.sn: Latin: 'Scripture alone.' The Protestant principle that the Bible is the sole infallible rule of faith and practice. This does not mean Scripture is t

docs.ti: sola fide (justification by faith alone)
docs.tk: sola_fide sola fide justification by faith alone latin  'by faith alone ' the central reformation doctrine that justification before god is received through faith  not earned by human merit or works  luther's key insight from romans 1 17 and 3 28  'for we hold that one is justified by faith apart from works of the law ' this was the material cause of the reformation   luther called it 'the article upon which the church stands or falls ' the council of trent  1547  formally anathematized sola fide  the debate often centers on james 2 24  'a person is justified by works and not by faith alone'   which protestants interpret as referring to the evidence of faith  not the ground of justification
docs.sn: Latin: 'by faith alone.' The central Reformation doctrine that justification before God is received through faith, not earned by human merit or works.

docs.ti: Filioque ('and the Son' — addition to the Nicene Creed)
docs.tk: filioque filioque 'and the son'   addition to the nicene creed latin  'and the son ' a clause added to the nicene creed by the western  latin  church  changing the phrase about the holy spirit from 'who proceeds from the father' to 'who proceeds from the father and the son ' first appeared in the third council of toledo  589 ad  and gradually spread through the western church  the eastern  greek  church rejected this addition as both theologically wrong  it subordinates the spirit and confuses the father's unique role as source origin  and canonically illegal  the creed was altered without an ecumenical council   this controversy was a major contributing factor to the great schism of 1054
docs.sn: Latin: 'and the Son.' A clause added to the Nicene Creed by the Western (Latin) church, changing the phrase about the Holy Spirit from 'who proceeds f

docs.ti: indulgentia (remission of temporal punishment for sin)
docs.tk: indulgence indulgentia remission of temporal punishment for sin in catholic practice  the remission of temporal punishment due for sins that have already been forgiven  based on the doctrine of the 'treasury of merit'   a spiritual reserve of the merits of christ  mary  and the saints that the church can dispense  originally  indulgences replaced harsh physical penances  by the late middle ages  they were sold for money to finance building projects  notably st  peter's basilica   johann tetzel's aggressive selling of indulgences provoked luther's 95 theses  1517  and sparked the reformation

docs.ti: Theotokos (God-bearer; Mother of God (title for Mary))

docs.ti: Yĕrûšālayim (foundation of peace; city of peace)
docs.tk: name_jerusalem yĕrûšālayim foundation of peace  city of peace most likely from yārāh  י ר ה  to establish found    šālôm  ש  לו ם  peace    'foundation of peace ' some derive it from an earlier canaanite form uru-salim  'city of  the god  shalem'   its earliest biblical name is salem  ש  ל ם  genesis 14 18   where melchizedek   priest of god most high   ruled  the dual ending -ayim may suggest the 'two jerusalems'  earthly and heavenly  galatians 4 26  revelation 21 2   the city whose name means 'peace' has been conquered  destroyed  and fought over more than any other city on earth   making the eschatological promise of true shalom all the more powerful
docs.sn: Most likely from yārāh (יָרָה, to establish/found) + šālôm (שָׁלוֹם, peace) = 'Foundation of Peace.' Some derive it from an earlier Canaanite form Uru

docs.ti: Šĕʾār Yāšûḇ (a remnant shall return)
docs.tk: shear_jashub šĕʾār yāšûḇ a remnant shall return isaiah's firstborn son  whose name is a walking prophecy  'a remnant shall return ' isaiah brought this child to meet king ahaz during the syro-ephraimite crisis  isaiah 7 3   making the boy's very presence a divine message  assyria would devastate  but a remnant would survive and return  the name combines shĕʾār  ש  א ר  remnant    yāšûḇ  י ש ו ב  shall return   this remnant theology becomes central to isaiah's message  isaiah 10 21-22  11 11  28 5  and is picked up by paul in romans 9 27 and 11 5  isaiah's son embodied hope in his very identity
docs.sn: Isaiah's firstborn son, whose name is a walking prophecy: 'A Remnant Shall Return.' Isaiah brought this child to meet King Ahaz during the Syro-Ephrai
